_comment: Room TYPES the player designates over a floored, walled area (Songs-of-Syx model). A room does nothing on its own — its output is the sum of the workstations placed inside it (see stations.json). Numeric ids are assigned by array order (0 = undesignated).

rooms.name: Home
rooms.desc: Sleeping quarters. Each bed or bunk inside houses settlers; warm when enclosed.

rooms.name: Kitchen
rooms.desc: Cooks raw grain into meals. Output scales with the ovens inside.

rooms.name: Bakery
rooms.desc: Bakes flour into bread. Needs baking ovens.

rooms.name: Mill
rooms.desc: Grinds grain into flour. Needs millstones.

rooms.name: Smithy
rooms.desc: Forges iron into tools and weapons. Needs anvils and weapon benches.

rooms.name: Foundry
rooms.desc: Smelts ore into iron. Needs smelters.

rooms.name: Sawmill
rooms.desc: Saws logs into timber. Needs saw benches.

rooms.name: Workshop
rooms.desc: Spins flax into rope and cloth. Needs looms and rope walks.

rooms.name: Kilnhouse
rooms.desc: Fires clay into brick and bakes coal into coke. Needs kilns and coke ovens.

rooms.name: Library
rooms.desc: Study and research. Desks give education; shelves store knowledge.

rooms.name: Infirmary
rooms.desc: Treats the sick and wounded. Each sickbed adds a care slot.

rooms.name: Apothecary
rooms.desc: Brews herbs into medicine. Needs herb tables.

rooms.name: Tavern
rooms.desc: Rest and recreation. Tables give recreation; brew vats make ale.

rooms.name: Storehouse
rooms.desc: Bulk storage. Each shelf adds stockpile capacity.

rooms.name: Burial Ground
rooms.desc: The colony's graveyard. Each grave marker gives one burial slot; without one the unburied dead drag on morale.

rooms.name: Outpost
rooms.desc: An open-air work post for hunters and rangers. Hunting lodges placed here send workers into the wilds for game.

rooms.name: Watchtower
rooms.desc: A tall sentry post. Watch posts inside give advance warning of approaching raids and reduce the colony's chance of being caught off-guard.

rooms.name: Yard
rooms.desc: An open courtyard. Place wells here to provide clean water, lowering the colony's infection risk.

rooms.name: Market
rooms.desc: A trading square. Each market stall inside generates gold income each day from commerce with passing merchants.

rooms.name: Barracks
rooms.desc: Military quarters. Training posts inside keep settlers drilled; settlers who sleep here arm first during raids.

rooms.name: Pasture
rooms.desc: Grazing ground for livestock. Animal pens inside turn feed grain into dairy, a fresh food that broadens the diet.

rooms.name: Temple
rooms.desc: A house of worship. Shrines inside hold services that lift the spirits of the faithful.
